origin_prompt:
Design and implement a horizontal side‑scrolling adventure game using Unity (2022 LTS or newer) with C# scripts, targeting Windows 10/11. The project must be exportable through Unity Hub as a complete Unity folder and generate a runnable .exe file. The core gameplay features a minimalist character, represented by a Unity 2D sprite, positioned constantly on a fixed horizontal line on the left side of the screen and moving continuously to the right. Along the path, horizontal line segments of varying heights appear as obstacles; these segments come in two size categories—short segments whose width does not exceed 1.5 times the character’s width, and long segments whose width is at least three times the character’s width. The player presses the space bar to make the character jump; a single jump suffices for short segments, while long segments require a double jump while airborne. The character’s speed increases by ten percent every thirty seconds, capping at three times the initial speed. Contact with any obstacle, including a failed jump, triggers a game‑over state.
The interface displays a timer in the upper‑right corner, showing the elapsed survival time with a precision of 0.01 seconds. Upon game over, the final score—equal to the survival time—is shown, and a “Restart” button appears on the main menu to begin a new session. Visual design uses a solid‑color gradient or simple texture for the background, with obstacle lines rendered in a contrasting hue such as red and given a subtle shadow for clarity. Short sound effects accompany jumping, collisions, speed boosts, and the game‑over event, drawn from free open‑source libraries.
Deliverables include the full Unity project compressed into a .zip file containing all scripts, scenes, assets, and configuration files, as well as a directly executable Windows .exe together with its accompanying Data folder. Documentation comprises a PDF game design specification detailing functional overview, system architecture diagram, key script explanations, resource inventory, and the Unity version used; a DOCX quick‑start guide covering installation, execution, controls, and FAQs; and a PPTX presentation intended for non‑technical stakeholders, highlighting core mechanics, visual style, and implementation highlights. Quality expectations require all scripts to compile without warnings, a stable frame rate of at least 60 FPS on a typical PC, and an application launch time not exceeding five seconds. The entire scope must be completed and all materials submitted within fourteen days from the start date, delivered via email or a file‑sharing service such as Google Drive, with the archive named using the pattern “SideScrollerAdventure_<submission date>.zip”.

full_tree.requirements: A complete Unity project folder (including all scenes, scripts, assets) that opens without errors in Unity 2022 LTS or newer.

sub_tasks.requirements: C# scripts compile without warnings and implement character movement, obstacle generation, jump logic, speed scaling, and collision handling.
Gameplay mechanics (single jump for short segments, double jump for long segments, speed increase every 30 s up to 3×) function exactly as described.

full_tree.requirements: A built Windows 10/11 executable (.exe) with accompanying Data folder that launches in ≤5 seconds and runs at a stable ≥60 FPS on a typical PC.

sub_tasks.requirements: Executable starts the game, displays main menu with Restart button, timer, and runs the side‑scrolling gameplay.
Game‑over state triggers UI showing final survival time and offers a functional Restart option.

full_tree.requirements: User interface elements present and display correct information.

sub_tasks.requirements: Timer displayed in upper‑right corner with 0.01 s precision during gameplay.
Final score equals survival time and is shown on the game‑over screen.

full_tree.requirements: Audio assets integrated and triggered appropriately without clipping.

sub_tasks.requirements: Jump, collision, speed‑boost, and game‑over sound effects play at the correct moments.

full_tree.requirements: Documentation package delivered as a ZIP named SideScrollerAdventure_<date>.zip containing all required deliverables.

sub_tasks.requirements: PDF design specification with functional overview, system architecture diagram, script explanations, resource inventory, and Unity version.
DOCX quick‑start guide covering installation, execution, controls, and FAQs.
PPTX presentation for non‑technical stakeholders highlighting core mechanics, visual style, and implementation highlights.

full_tree.requirements: Project delivery compliance with timeline and delivery method.

sub_tasks.requirements: All deliverables submitted within 14 days from start date via email or file‑sharing service.
Archive includes both the Unity project folder and the built Windows executable with Data folder.
